TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: 001 izua aya tua ku milimilingan, izua aya zua maraɫak a ti

S.FORM: izua maḍusaḍusa selapay nu nasepuɫu aravats, aya.
izua madrusadrusa selapay nu nasepulju aravac, aya.
S.TRANSL: With only the two of them they were extremely lonely, they say.

S.FORM: "qai, qunu, qai mai sapuɫu itjen.
"qai, qunu, qai mai sapulju itjen.
S.TRANSL: “Well, son, we are lonely.

S.FORM: qai muri tjemavang itjen tua vaung tua lizuk tua gadu," aya tiamadju a maraɫak, aya.
qai muri tjemavang itjen tua vaung tua lizuk tua gadu," aya tiamadju a maraljak, aya.
S.TRANSL: Now we’ll make the sea, the plains and the mountains,” said the parent and child, they say.

S.FORM: manu izuanga nakipalinguɫ a vaung.
manu izuanga nakipalingulj a vaung.
S.TRANSL: So then there was the sea all around.

S.FORM: izuanga tiamadju itua tjaɫavavavan a gadu a tjemavatjavang tua vaung tua lizuk tua gadu, aya.
izuanga tiamadju itua tjaljavavavan a gadu a tjemavatjavang tua vaung tua lizuk tua gadu, aya.
S.TRANSL: There they were on the highest mountain making the sea, the plains and the mountains, they say.

S.FORM: "qai qai, muri puɫauɫaung itjen tua maretimaɫimaɫi a kasiw a venutsiatsiar a venangavangaɫ tua kakanen a mareka kaizuazua," aya uta, aya.
"qai qai, muri puljauljaung itjen tua maretimaljimalji a kasiw a venuciaciar a venangavangalj tua kakanen a mareka kaizuazua," aya uta, aya.
S.TRANSL: “Well we’ll get shade from the various kinds of trees that have blossom and bear fruit for food of all kinds(?),” they said too, they say.

S.FORM: manu izuanga uta naseleman anga a gadu a kasiw matatukalulum anga matatuzarzarzar anga vutsiar, aya.
manu izuanga uta naseleman anga a gadu a kasiw matatukalulum anga matatuzarzarzar anga vuciar, aya.
S.TRANSL: And so the mountains became shaded (?); trees got ripe in turn and the blossoms became bright in turn, they say.

S.FORM: nuka tsemel anga tsinuvuqan anga sepeɫuq a kana qipuqipu anga, aya.
nuka cemel anga cinuvuqan anga sepeljuq a kana qipuqipu anga, aya.
S.TRANSL: Grass too sprang up, and all the ground was full up, they say.

S.FORM: manu "qai muri pukuku itjen, qunu, tua qemuzimuzip tua qayaqayam tua ɫemanguɫanguy i taladj tua vaung," manu aya uta tiamadju a maraɫak, aya.
manu "qai muri pukuku itjen, qunu, tua qemuzimuzip tua qayaqayam tua ljemanguljanguy i taladj tua vaung," manu aya uta tiamadju a maraljak, aya.
S.TRANSL: Then “Now we’ll have pets, child; animals, birds and things that swim in the sea,” the parent and child also said, they say.

S.FORM: saka tjikezem, manu ka tsimeraw izuanga uta; nekanga nuka ita ika izua.
saka tjikezem, manu ka cimeraw izuanga uta; nekanga nuka ita ika izua.
S.TRANSL: So they closed their eyes, and when they opened them, there it all was; there was not a thing missing;

S.FORM: nekanga nu kinaqusaw tua kuku, aya.
nekanga nu kinaqusaw tua kuku, aya.
S.TRANSL: they weren’t missing any pets, they say.

S.FORM: saka izuanga tiamadju a talidulidu sakamaya, aya.
saka izuanga tiamadju a talidulidu sakamaya, aya.
S.TRANSL: So there they were just enjoying themselves, they say.

S.FORM: "qai, qunu, djavadjavay ɫakua ku pai sa-u ḍi, ɫizavu a tja sikataqaɫan i tjariteku," ayain ti Pulelelelengan nua zua kama ti Kuɫeɫeɫeɫe, aya.
"qai, qunu, djavadjavay ljakua ku pai sa-u dri, ljizavu a tja sikataqaljan i tjariteku," ayain ti Pulelelelengan nua zua kama ti Kuljeljeljelje, aya.
S.TRANSL: “Now, child, I’m sorry but off you go and see our fellows in the world below,” said father Kuɫeɫeɫeɫe to Pulelelelengan, they say.

S.FORM: "manu ika na ku ɫizaven ḍi, kama, ki kemudakuda?" aya ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
"manu ika na ku ljizaven dri, kama, ki kemudakuda?" aya ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
S.TRANSL: So “If I go to see them,(?) father, what am I to do?” said Pulelelelengan, they say.

S.FORM: qau "ui, djavadjavay, ɫakua sa-u kivakini ki pasemalaw a tja sikataqaɫan, qunu.
qau "ui, djavadjavay, ljakua sa-u kivakini ki pasemalaw a tja sikataqaljan, qunu.
S.TRANSL: “Yes, I’m sorry, but go; our fellows will tell you, child.

S.FORM: ɫakua maya patjekutjekuɫ tua tjelu a puluq a qadaw, ḍi, sa su pangtjez sa su kitsepeliw a sema maza," aya ti Kuɫeɫeɫeɫe tua zua aɫak a ti Pulelelelengan a pazazekazekatj, aya.
ljakua maya patjekutjekulj tua tjelu a puluq a qadaw, dri, sa su pangtjez sa su kicepeliw a sema maza," aya ti Kuljeljeljelje tua zua aljak a ti Pulelelelengan a pazazekazekatj, aya.
S.TRANSL: But don’t stay longer than 30 days and come back here,” ordered Kuɫeɫeɫeɫe to his child Pulelelelengan, they say.

S.FORM: sa vaik ti Pulelelelengan a ɫemizaw tua i tjariteku, aya.
sa vaik ti Pulelelelengan a ljemizaw tua i tjariteku, aya.
S.TRANSL: So Pulelelelengan went to visit the world below, they say.

S.FORM: manu zemuker i tjariteku maka tjaia Muatjukutjuku azua ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
manu zemuker i tjariteku maka tjaia Muatjukutjuku azua ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
S.TRANSL: And Pulelelelengan arrived in the world below by way of the Muatjukutjuku house, they say.

S.FORM: manu ka zemuker anga, "keɫu, ɫa sikataqaɫan.
manu ka zemuker anga, "kelju, lja sikataqaljan.
S.TRANSL: And when he had arrived, he kept saying: “Come here, fellow villagers,

S.FORM: qa keɫu satjaiqayavu tjanuaken.
qa kelju satjaiqayavu tjanuaken.
S.TRANSL: come to me.

S.FORM: ku nu tjautsikelay ku nu pasemalavay," ayaya timadju.
ku nu tjaucikelay ku nu pasemalavay," ayaya timadju.
S.TRANSL: I will speak to you and explain to you.”

S.FORM: sa qepuqepu-i(?) a setjariteku, sa pasekeɫangi tua nanguaqan nua kinaizuanan, tua izua kama nimadju a tjaɫakiɫivakan, nu ayaya, qai ka sekeɫang anga a i tjariteku, "qu manu kemuda a nu izua tjananguaq tua tja kinaizuanan, a nu izua rukiɫivakan aravats," aya zua setjariteku, aya.

sa qepuqepu-i(') a setjariteku, sa pasekeljangi tua nanguaqan nua kinaizuanan, tua izua kama nimadju a tjaljakiljivakan, nu ayaya, qai ka sekeljang anga a i tjariteku, "qu manu kemuda a nu izua tjananguaq tua tja kinaizuanan, a nu izua rukiljivakan aravac," aya zua setjariteku, aya.
S.TRANSL: So he gathered the people of the world below, and explained to them the goodness of his place, and about his most loving father. And when the people of the world below understood this, they said: “So that’s how it is; so there is a place better than our place, and there is someone really loving,” said the people of the world below, they say.

S.FORM: saka a(?) mapuzangal anga tua patsun tua sema tjarivavaw, aya.
saka a(') mapuzangal anga tua pacun tua sema tjarivavaw, aya.
S.TRANSL: And they longed to see and to get to the world above, they say.

S.FORM: "qai, ɫa sikataqaɫan, patjatjautsiketsikelu anga ḍi, pasekeɫangu anga a ika nakiqepu a ika ku sintsevungan(?) ḍi; uɫa mun a kemɫang a mapuɫat tua kiɫivak ni kama, tua nanguaqan nua i tjarivavaw," aya ti Kuɫe-Pulelelelengan.

"qai, lja sikataqaljan, patjatjaucikecikelu anga dri, pasekeljangu anga a ika nakiqepu a ika ku sincevungan(') dri; ulja mun a kemljang a mapuljat tua kiljivak ni kama, tua nanguaqan nua i tjarivavaw," aya ti Kulje-Pulelelelengan.
S.TRANSL: “Fellow villagers, tell one another; explain to the people who didn’t turn up, whom I haven’t met; so that you will all know the love of my father and the goodness of the world above,” said Kuɫe-Pulelelelengan.

S.FORM: sa vaik a kitsepeliw a sema tua kama i tjarivavaw, aya.
sa vaik a kicepeliw a sema tua kama i tjarivavaw, aya.
S.TRANSL: And he went back to his father in the world above, they say.

S.FORM: sa tjautsiketsikel anga tua kama a ɫemitaɫita tua kinaitjaritekuan ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
sa tjaucikecikel anga tua kama a ljemitaljita tua kinaitjaritekuan ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
S.TRANSL: And Pulelelelengan told his father everything that he had done in the world below, they say.

S.FORM: selapay nu namalevaleva aravats a "manu izua nanguaq matu sa tja kinaizuanan, manu zua izua mamazangiɫan aravats a rukiɫivak," aya setjariteku.
selapay nu namalevaleva aravac a "manu izua nanguaq matu sa tja kinaizuanan, manu zua izua mamazangiljan aravac a rukiljivak," aya setjariteku.
S.TRANSL: And the people were very glad. “So there is something good like our own place. And there is a real chief who is truly loving,” said the people of the world below,

S.FORM: saka namalevanga aravats, saka sa su u iravaravanga sa su(?) kalevalevanga tua sema tjarivavaw a kitjaviliɫ tua ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
saka namalevanga aravac, saka sa su u iravaravanga sa su(') kalevalevanga tua sema tjarivavaw a kitjavililj tua ti Pulelelelengan, aya.
S.TRANSL: and they were very glad, “So get ready and be glad to go to the world above following Pulelelelengan,” they said (?).
